message:
perf: eliminate unnecessary string allocations in case-insensitive comparisons

Replace `to_ascii_lowercase()` calls that allocate new strings with
byte-level `eq_ignore_ascii_case()` comparisons and the existing
`contains_ci` helper. This avoids heap allocations on every comparison
in reply subject prefix detection (cli, messaging), timeline search
filtering (console), recipient filtering (tui_compose), and the
similarity_score LCS loop (tools).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -344,7 +344,7 @@ impl ComposeState {
         self.recipients
             .iter()
             .enumerate()
-            .filter(|(_, r)| r.name.to_ascii_lowercase().contains(&filter_lower))
+            .filter(|(_, r)| crate::tui_screens::contains_ci(&r.name, &filter_lower))
             .map(|(i, _)| i)
             .collect()
     }
